THREE TREES APPROXIMATELY 12 TO 18 INCHES TWISTED AND SNAPPED. ALSO RECEIVED DIME HAIL AND APPROXIMATELY 1.25 INCHES OF RAIN.
DEPUTY REPORTS 18 TO 20 POWER POLES SNAPPED ALONG THE HIGHWAY WEST OF MINNEWAUKEN.
DEPUTY REPORTS 18 TO 20 POWER POLES SNAPPED ALONG THE HIGHWAY WEST OF MINNEWAUKEN. PHOTO SHOWS AN APPARENT MICROBURST WITH VERY STRONG OUTFLOW. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 110 MPH.
QUARTER SIZED HAIL IN WADENA.
NUMEROUS LARGE TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS WERE BROKEN DOWN ACROSS SOUTH CENTRAL GRAND FORKS. A LARGE TREE WAS SNAPPED JUST SOUTH OF DOWNTOWN.
QUARTER SIZED HAIL IN WADENA.
SEVERAL LARGE 4 TO 8 INCH DIAMETER TREE BARNCHES BROKEN DOWN IN VARIOUS HOMESTEAD YARDS. ONE 12 INCH DIAMETER BOX ELDER TREE WAS SNAPPED.

SEVERAL LARGE 4 TO 8 INCH DIAMTER TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS WERE BROKEN DOWN AT VARIOUS FARMSTEADS IN WESTERN ANGUS TOWNSHIP. A FEW 10 DIAMTER POPLAR TREES WERE SNAPPED. BLOWING DUST AND OLD CROP DEBRIS REDUCED VISBILITIES FOR BIREF PERIODS OF TIME.
A FARM DRIVE-THROUGH SHED WAS BLOWN DOWN. SEVERAL LARGE TREE BRANCHES WERE ALSO BROKEN DOWN. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR AND NEARBY MESONET OBSERVATIONS.

A FEW LARGE 4 TO 8 INCH DIAMETER TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS WERE BROKEN DOWN IN FARMSTEADS ACROSS SOUTHEAST SANDERS TOWNSHIP. TIN ROOFING WAS BLOWN OFF OF ONE FARM OUTBUILDING. SOME CATTLE PEN FENCING WAS BLOWN DOWN AT ANOTHER FARMSTEAD.

THIS TORNADO WAS ORIGINALLY REPORTED BY GFK/ATC AND GFAFB OFFICIALS. A DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND THAT THE TORNADO TRACKED TO THE EAST-NORTHEAST OVER LARGELY OPEN FIELDS. IT LIKELY TRACKED FOR LESS THAN HALF A MILE AND DISSIPATED BY 1252 PM CDT. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 70 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 25 YARDS.
REPORTED BY ATC GFK...QUICKLY DISSIPATED.

THIS TORNADO WAS VIEWED BY GFK ATC FOR NEARLY 11 MINUTES UNTIL ABOUT 110 PM CDT AND WAS INITIALLY ESTIMATED AT 7 SW GFK. THIS TORNADO TRACKED NEARLY 3 MILES FROM ABOUT 2 WSW OF HATTON TO AROUND 1N OF HATTON BY 110 PM CDT. THE TORNADO HAD A DRAMATIC DUST SWIRL WHICH WAS CAPTURED ON VIDEO AND PHOTOS. SOME GROUND SCARRING WAS EVIDENT, OTHERWISE NO DAMAGE WAS DETECTED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 75 YARDS.

TORNADO TRACKED FOR 2.7 MILES TO AROUND 1 MILE NORTH OF HATTON BY 108 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO TRACKED WITHIN A MILE OF HATTON BUT STAYED MAINLY OVER OPENED FIELDS. FIELDS SHOWED SOME GROUND SCARRING BUT OTHERWISE NO SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS DETECTED.
THIS TORNADO WAS VIEWED BY WEATHER OBSERVERS AT GFAFB AND GFK AIRPORT UNTIL AROUND 110 PM CDT.

THIS TORNADO APPARENTLY TOUCHED DOWN AROUND 2 MILES SOUTH OF ARVILLA AND TRACKED VERY SLOWLY TO THE ENE ACROSS CR2 FOR ABOUT A QUARTER OF A MILE, ENDING BY 104 PM CDT. NO SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS DETECTED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 70 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 50 YARDS.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AND TRACKED FOR AROUND 1.5 MILES TO ABOUT 3 MILES NORTH OF MAYVILLE BY 118 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO WAS VIEWED BY LOCAL RESIDENTS AND THE TOUCHDOWN WAS PHOTOGRAPHED BY OBSERVERS ON I-29, MORE THAN 11 MILES DUE EAST OF THE TOUCHDOWN LOCATION. THOUGH THE TALL DUST SWIRL CROSSED CR14 THERE WAS NO SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE DETECTED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 75 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 100 YARDS.

A FUNNEL CLOUD WAS REPORTED BY GFAFB OBSERVERS WEST-SOUTHWEST OF THEIR LOCATION. A DAMAGE SURVEY NOTED AN APPARENT BRIEF TOUCHDOWN WITH BROKEN BRACHES IN A SHELTER BELT JUST SOUTH OF HIGHWAY 2 AND WITH BRANCH DEBRIS FLUNG EASTWARD INTO THE ADJACENT FIELD. THE TORNADO TRACKED EAST-NORTHEAST FOR ABOUT ONE HALF MILE AND ENDED BY 119 PM CDT. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 75 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 100 YARDS.

THIS TORNADO TRACK EAST-NORTHEAST FOR NEARLY 4 MILES TO AROUND 7 NORTH OF MAYVILLE BY 128 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO PRODUCED A TALL DUST SWIRL BUT LITTLE TO NO APPARENT DAMAGE. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 75 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 100 YARDS.

A DEPUTY REPORTED A TOUCHDOWN AROUND 2 MILES SOUTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF GF CR3 AND CR6. THE TORNADO APPEARED TO TRACKED EASTWARD FOR ABOUT A MILE OVER A 2 TO 3 MINUTE SPAN AND ENDED BY 121 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO WAS VIEWED AND REPORTED BY GFAFB OBSERVERS AND WAS ALSO VIDEOED FROM A POINT SOUTH OF GFK LOOKING WEST. THOUGH THE TORNADO TRACKED OVER FIELDS AND SHELTER BELTS THERE WAS NO SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE NOTED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 70 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 100 YARDS.
BRIEF TOUCHDOWN NOTED AND PHOTO POSTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. LOCATION APPROXIMATE BASED ON RADAR AND EARLIER FUNNEL REPORT CLOUD REPORTS.

THIS TORNADO TRACKED TO THE NORTHEAST FOR ABOUT 2 MILES TO AROUND 1 SSW OF HOLMES BY 130 PM CDT. NO SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS DETECTED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 75 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 75 YARDS.
BRIEF TOUCHDOWN NOTED NEAR PORTLAND JUNCTION.
NUMEROUS POPLAR AND ASH TREES BROKEN DOWN IN SHELTER BELTS ACROSS SOUTHERN LEVANT TOWNSHIP.
NUMEROUS POPLAR TREES BROKEN DOWN IN SHELTER BELTS WEST OF TOWN.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT.
NUMEROUS POPLAR TREES WERE SNAPPED IN SHELTER BELTS AND FARM YARDS ACROSS EASTERN WALSHVILLE AND PULASKI TOWNSHIPS.
A FEW DIME TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL.
REPORTED 3 ENE BAKER
TREES DOWN IN THE CITY OF NEWFOLDEN.

PEAK WIND MEASURED BY AN AGRICULTURAL WEATHER STATION AT THE CANADIAN BORDER. NEARBY CORN AND SMALL GRAIN FIELDS WERE STRIPPED BY WIND DRIVEN HAIL. EVERGREEN AND POPLAR TREES WERE TOPPLED AND STEEL GRAIN BINS WERE POPS IN VARIOUS FARMSTEADS ACROSS BYRON TOWNSHIP.

A TORNADO ENGULFED IN DOWNBURST WINDS AND HAIL TRACKED FOR NEARLY TWO MILES ENDING AROUND 3.5 MILES ESE OF WALES BY 612 PM CDT. THE TORNADO SNAPPED NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS AND BRANCHES ALONG A PATH THROUGH MULTIPLE SHELTER BELTS. NEARBY FIELDS WERE STRIPPED OF CROPS BY WIND DRIVEN HAIL. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 85 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 200 YARDS.
NUMEROUS LARGE 4 TO 8 INCH BRANCHES BROKEN DOWN IN THE WIND AT FARMSTEAD.

A WEAK TORNADO TRACKED TO THE SOUTHEAST FOR AROUND 1.25 MILES AND LIFTED BY 629 PM CDT. THE TORNADO BLEW THE DOORS OFF A SEED COMPANY ELEVATOR AND SPREAD DOOR AND ROOF PANELS AROUND THE GROUNDS IN A CIRCULAR PATTERN. FURTHER DOWNSTREAM THE TORNADO SNAPPED 6 POWER POLES ALONG 96TH AVE NE... A QUARTER MILE EAST OF HIGHWAY 1. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 105 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 600 YARDS.
PEAK WIND MEASURED AT THE NDAWN STATION EAST OF TOWN.

A TORNADO WAS PARTIALLY ENGULFED IN DOWNBURST WINDS AND HAIL AS IT TRACKED FOR NEARLY 20 MILES TO AROUND 3 MILES S OF MOUNTAIN BY 715 PM CDT. LARGE WOODEN POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED, ROOFING WAS STRIPPED OFF OF HOMES, STEEL POLE SHEDS WERE TOTALLY DIMANTLED, NUMEROUS STEEL GRAIN BINS WERE CRUSHED AND LARGE METAL SEGMENTS WERE CARRIED FOR A MILE OR MORE DOWNWIND. BEAN AND GRAIN FILEDS WERE COMPLETELY STRIPPED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 120 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 800 YARDS.
LARGE PORTIONS OF ALMA AND EAST ALMA TOWNSHIPS WERE STRIPPED OF SMALL GRAIN AND BEAN CROPS BY EXTREME DOWNBURST WINDS AND WIND DRIVEN HAIL.

A TORNADO TRACKED IN AN EAST-SOUTHEAST DIRECTION ALONG A ROUGHLY 8 MILE INTERMITTENT TRACK TO AROUND 2 WNW OF HARLOW BY 720 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO AND CORRESPONDING REAR FLANK DOWNDRAFT WIND PRODUCED WIDESPREAD DUST WHICH GREATLY REDUCED VISIBILITY. NUMEROUS ASH, POPLAR, COTTONWOOD, AND EVERGREEN TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED IN SHELTER BELTS AND GROVES ALONG ITS PATH. PEAK WINDS OF 119 MPH WERE MEASURED BY A MOBILE SYSTEM. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 600 YARDS.
NUMEROUS LARGE TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS BROKEN DOWN AROUND ACROSS MUCH OF THINGVALLA TOWNSHIP. SOME TREES SNAPPED. SHEET METAL ROOFING AND SHINGLES BLOWN OFF BUILDINGS IN VARIOUS LOCATIONS.
SEVERAL BUILDINGS DOWN,TYPE AND EXTENT OF DAMAGE UNKNOWN AT PRESENT.
SEVERAL ROOFS BLOWN OFF BUILDINGS.
EXTENSIVE DAMAGE IN MILTON. UNQUANTIFIED AT THIS TIME.
BRIEF TOUCHDOWN NOTED WEST OF HARLOW.
MEASURED ON MOBILE MESONET EQUIPMENT.
MEASURED ON MOBILE MESONET EQUIPMENT.
LARGE PINE TREE BLOWN DOWN.
PEAK WINDS MEASURED AT A PERSONAL WEATHER STATION.
PEAK WINDS MEASURED AT A PERSONAL WEATHER STATION. NUMEROUS OAK, POPLAR, AND PINE TREES SNAPPED IN SHELTER BELTS ACROSS ELDORA AND CRYSTAL TOWNSHIPS.
PEAK WINDS MEASURED AT A PERSONAL WEATHER STATION.
REPORTED 3 ENE BAKER

A TORNADO TRACKED EASTWARD OVER LARGELY OPEN COUNTRY FOR NEARLY 2 MILES TO AROUND 4 NNW OF MINNEWAUKAN BY AROUND 737 PM CDT. THE GROUND SWIRL OF THIS TORNADO WAS VIDEOED BY STORM CHASERS NEAR THE WEST BAY OF DEVILS LAKE THOUGH VERY DUSTY CONDITION MADE DETECTION DIFFICULT. NO SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS DETECTED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 200 YARDS.
PEAK WINDS MEASURED AT THE ST. THOMAS NDAWN STATION.
PEEAK WINDS MEASURED AT THE ST. THOMAS NDAWN STATION.
VERY STRONG WINDS AND VERY HEAVY RAIN WITH DIME TO NICKEL SIZED HAIL.
EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE EXTENDING FROM EASTERN FARMINGTON INTO MARTIN TOWNSHIP. NUMEROUS LARGE POPLAR, ASH, AND COTTONWOODS WERE SNAPPED IN FIELD AND FARMSTEAD SHELTER BELTS ACROSS THIS AREA.
MULTIPLE TREES HAVE BEEN DOWNED.
DAVIS MEASUREMENT.
NDAWN MEASUREMENT.
A STEEL GRAIN BIN WAS BLOWN OFF ITS FOUNDATIOM AND TUMBLED FOR A QUARTER OF A MILE.

NUMEROUS LARGE POPLAR, ASH, AND COTTONWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED OR BLOWN DOWN IN SHELTER BELTS AND FARMSTEADS ACROSS ST. ANDREWS AND NORTHERN ACTON TOWNSHIPS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED FROM 85 TO 95 MPH OVER A SEVERAL MILE WIDE PATH.
TOUCHDOWN NOTED BY OBSERVER NEAR VINING OIL...LOOKING NORTH.
DVL RWIS
PEAK WINDS MEASURED AT THE CRARY NDAWN STATION.

A WEAK TORNADO TRACK TO EAST FOR NEARLY 3 MILES ACROSS FAR SOUTHERN EDDY TOWNSHIP ENDING ABOUT 7 SW OF HAMAR BY 833 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO CROSS THE SOUTHERN END ON LAKE WASHINGTON WHERE A CONDENSATION PLUME WAS ONSERVED. LARGE BRANCHES AND LIMBS WERE BROKEN OFF OF TREES ON EITHER SIDE OF THE LAKE, OTHERWISE NO SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS NOTED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 85 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 150 YARDS.

REPORT OF TOUCHDOWN SOUTHWEST OF WALUM VIEWED BY SPOTTERS NORTHEAST OF WIMBLEDON AND ALSO BY SPOTTERS NORTH OF DAZEY. A PERSISTENT WALLCLOUD STRUCTURE WITH FUNNEL-LIKE APPENDAGE WAS VIEWED FOR SEVERAL MINUTES AS IT PASSED ALONG THE BARNES AND GRIGGS COUNTY LINE. THOUGH A BRIEF DUST SWIRL WAS DETECTED NO SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS APPARENT. PATH LENGTH ESTIMATED AT ONE QUARTER OF A MILE...ENDING BY 933 PM CDT. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 70 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 75 YARDS.

THIS TORNADO SNAPPED OR UPROOTED NUMEROUS OAK AND PINE TREES, BLEW IN PART OF A WALL ON A BARN AND TORE THE ROOF OFF OF A POLE SHED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 105 MPH. VIDEO AND PHOTOGRAPHIC EVIDENCE SHOW THAT THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AND TRACKED TO THE EAST SOUTHEAST OVER OPEN FIELDS FOR A HALF MILE OF LESS...AND ENDED BY AROUND 625 PM CDT. NO DAMAGE WAS NOTED THOUGH A SIZABLE DUST PLUME WAS GENERATED. REPORT WAS FIRST MADE VIA BROADCAST AND SOCIAL MEDIA. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 70 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 75 YARDS.

THE TORNADO TRACKED EASTWARD FOR NEARLY 4 MILES AND ENDED ABOUT 1 SW OF HASTINGS BY 447 PM CDT. THE TORNADO PEELED SHINGLES AND ROOFING OFF OF HOMES AT TWO FARMSTEADS ROUGHLY A MILE APART... AND SNAPPED OR UPROOTED SEVERAL BOXELDER AND SPRUCE TREES AT BOTH FARMSTEADS. BRANCH SCATTER THROUGH A SHELTER BELT LOCATED MIDWAY BETWEEN THE FARMS SUGGESTED A POSSIBLE SECONDARY VORTEX. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 110 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 200 YARDS.

FACEBOOK REPORT INDICATING NUMEROUS LARGE BRANCHES SCATTERED ALL OVER THE ROAD FOR AROUND ONE EIGTH OF A MILE HALF WAY BETWEEN PAGE AND GARDNER ON COUNTY ROAD 26. BRANCHES ESTIMATED TO BE BLOWN OFF 100 FOOT TALL TREES WITH SOME BRANCHES AT LEAST 6 INCHES IN DIAMETER AND AT LEAST 10 FEET LONG. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
CELLULAR COMMUNICATIONS TOWER RECEIVED DAMAGE TO MOUNTED TRANSMITTER AND RECEIVER PLATFORMS. TECHNICIAN ESTIMATES WINDS IN EXCESS OF 80 MPH REQUIRED TO PRODUCE THE DAMAGE.

THE TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY NORTHWARD ALONG THE HIGHWAY 9 CORRIDOR FOR ABOUT 4 MILES AND ENDED JUST PAST THE COMMUNITY OF BELTRAMI BY 801 PM CDT. AS THE NASCENT TORNADO CROSSED FROM NORMAN COUNTY INTO POLK COUNTY DEBRIS BROKE WINDOWS IN A VEHICLE. THE TORNADO THEN SNAPPED TREES IN AND AROUND THE COMMUNITY OF BELTAMI AND DISMANTLED A METAL WRAPPED POLESHED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 105 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 100 YARDS.

NUMEROUS 4 TO 6 INCH DIAMETER TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS WERE BROKEN DOWN IN SHELTER BELTS ACROSS SOUTHERN POPLAR RIVER TOWNSHIP. THE NEARBY RWIS STATION LOST POWER AT THIS TIME PERIOD.
